title: Daily Market Digest
subtitle: March 12, 2026 - Geopolitical Tensions Drive Oil Higher, Markets Mixed

items.headline: Oil Surge and Geopolitical Concerns Lead to Mixed Market Close
items.summary: U.S. stock markets closed mixed on Thursday, March 12, as elevated crude oil prices, fueled by the ongoing Middle East conflict, heightened inflation fears and kept investors cautious. The Dow and S&P 500 ended in negative territory, while the Nasdaq Composite managed a slight gain. Early trading saw steeper declines across all major indices as Brent crude briefly surpassed $100 per barrel.

items.headline: Oracle Jumps on Strong AI Cloud Demand
items.summary: Oracle Corp. ($ORCL) surged 9.2% after reporting stronger-than-expected Q3 FY26 earnings and revenue, driven by explosive growth in cloud infrastructure for AI workloads.

items.headline: KPI Green Energy Ltd. Shows Robust Intraday Performance
items.summary: KPI Green Energy Ltd. (N/A) saw an 8.36% surge, hitting an intraday high of Rs 394.05, bucking the broader market trend and signaling strength within the power sector.

items.headline: Sable Offshore Corp. Leads March Gainers
items.summary: Sable Offshore Corp. ($SOC) led monthly performance gainers for March, up 92.79% as of March 12, 2026.

items.headline: United Airlines Tumbles on High Fuel Costs
items.summary: United Airlines ($UAL) plummeted 33.7% as concerns over rising crude oil prices directly impacted companies with significant fuel bills.

items.headline: Piramal Pharma Among Top Losers
items.summary: Piramal Pharma (N/A) was one of the worst performers, falling 6.36% to ₹145.54, its lowest level since June 2024.

items.headline: Dollar General Drops on Weak Forecast
items.summary: Dollar General ($DG) shares tumbled 7.8% after the retailer provided a weaker-than-expected forecast for revenue growth in the upcoming fiscal year.

items.headline: Consumer Staples Outperform Tech Amid Defensive Pivot
items.summary: A significant sector rotation is underway, with investors moving out of high-growth technology stocks and into the more defensive Consumer Staples sector. The Consumer Staples Select Sector SPDR Fund ($XLP) has gained 13.2% year-to-date, becoming the top-performing S&P 500 sector, while the Technology sector ($XLK) has seen a 4.6% loss. This indicates a shift in investor psychology towards predictable earnings and inflation-resistant dividends.

items.headline: February CPI Aligns with Expectations; Fed Rate Hike Pause Expected
items.summary: The February Consumer Price Index (CPI) report showed headline inflation at 2.4% year-over-year and core CPI at 2.5%, largely in line with forecasts. Prediction markets indicate a 99% conviction that the Federal Reserve will hold interest rates steady at its March 17-18 meeting, with market expectations narrowing to just one rate cut for all of 2026, likely in December.

items.headline: Crude Oil Prices Remain Elevated Amid Middle East Tensions
items.summary: Crude oil prices continued to climb due to severe supply concerns stemming from the ongoing conflict in the Middle East, particularly Iranian attacks on commercial shipping in the Strait of Hormuz. Brent crude briefly spiked above $100 per barrel before settling lower. The International Energy Agency (IEA) announced a release of 400 million barrels from strategic reserves to help calm markets.

items.headline: Adobe and USA Rare Earth Earnings on Watch
items.summary: Investors are closely monitoring earnings reports today from Adobe ($ADBE) and USA Rare Earth for insights into the technology and critical minerals sectors. Earlier, Campbell's reported weaker-than-expected profit and cut its forecasts, leading to a 7.1% stock decline.

items.headline: Power and Renewable Energy Sectors Outperform
items.summary: Despite broader market weakness, power and renewable energy stocks demonstrated strong gains. Companies like NTPC Green Energy, Adani Power, and JSW Energy were among the top performers of the day, with NTPC Green Energy specifically up 11.84%.
